title: Tokens, Source Spans, Document Trees, Invariants, and Traversal · Changed-Input Lab
summary: An independent documentation team receives a different content tree, language, route base, template, asset set, trust policy, hosting target, and accessibility need and transfers tokens, source spans, document trees, invariants, and traversal into a changed typed source-mapped document tree. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of typed source-mapped document tree with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-cas…
objectives: Distinguish scanning, tokenization, block parsing, inline parsing, tree construction, transformation, validation, and rendering.
Model document, block, inline, text, code, link, image, and error nodes with explicit payload and children invariants.
Retain source path, line, column, byte or code-point span, and original excerpt through transformations.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Parse Phases
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab publishing team handles case 9311 during an accessibility-led community newsroom release. Build deterministic Python evidence to construct a source-mapped acyclic document tree under a depth budget; reconcile content, routes, navigation, links, assets, feeds, sitemaps, output files, and artifact digests before approval; then replace a trusted author fixture with untrusted raw HTML, unsafe URLs, and an active SVG. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Parse Phases.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for distinguish scanning, tokenization, block parsing, inline parsing, tree construction, transformation, validation, and rendering. For Static-site publishing and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Parse Phases, keep Parse Phases connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

options.text: State the Parse Phases preconditions, preserve earlier contracts, and name the trace or changed-input result that will decide success.
Parsing Markdown is a sequence of regular-expression replacements. That claim fails the commit to the mission model evidence boundary for Parse Phases.

For commit to the mission model, render one trusted short page at the root, omit content authority, source spans, parser limits, route base, URL and asset reconciliation, contextual encoding, accessibility, clean-build comparison, deployment, rollback, recovery, and production transfer, then accept plausible HTML as publication proof. This does not satisfy Parse Phases for typed source-mapped document tree.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-visitor-transform-1e6cvr
# Competency: ssg-visitor-transform.
# Case 2124: an offline-first field documentation build.
# Operating constraint: preserve source spans, Unicode policy, trust classification, route base, contextual encoding, diagnostics, and accessibility decisions.
# Changed case: delete a source and change a shared layout, then compare incremental and clean artifact digests.
def evidence_ssg_visitor_transform_1e6cvr(tokens: int = 18, nodes: int = 15, source_spans: int = 15, invalid_edges: int = 0, max_depth: int = 5) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1e6cvr = "1e6cvr-rvc6e1-2124"
    assert evidence_variant_1e6cvr
    evidence_axes_1e6cvr = (
        "reader1e6cvr",
        "source2124",
        "parservc6e1",
        "route1e6cvr2124",
        "artifact21241e6cvr",
        "recoveryrvc6e12124",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1e6cvr)) == 6
    if tokens < 1 or nodes < 1:
        return False, nodes, "tree-shape"
    if source_spans != nodes:
        return False, source_spans, "span-coverage"
    if invalid_edges > 0:
        return False, invalid_edges, "tree-invariant"
    if max_depth < 1 or max_depth > 64:
        return False, max_depth, "depth-budget"
    assert source_spans == nodes
    return True, nodes, "source-mapped-tree"
